page.layer4.description: TCP and UDP load balancers (NLBs) and upstream health.
defaultServer.title: Default site
defaultServer.subtitle: Catch-all nginx server and landing page for unmatched hosts or bare IP access.
defaultServer.readOnlyHint: Only administrators can write nginx config and index.html on the server.
defaultServer.nginxBlockDesc: Full server { ... } for port 80 (default_server). Invalid syntax is rejected; nginx is tested before reload.
defaultServer.indexHtmlDesc: Served from /var/www/html when the default server handles the request.
defaultServer.tipsDesc: Common patterns when nothing is configured yet.
defaultServer.tip1: Replace the server block with a single return 301 https://$host$request_uri; (or a fixed URL) if you want every unknown host to redirect.
defaultServer.tip2: Keep the /.well-known/acme-challenge/ location if you issue certificates via HTTP-01 validation.
defaultServer.toast.saved: Default server updated and nginx reloaded.
defaultServer.loadErrorHint: The API must run on the nginx host with permission to read /etc/nginx and /var/www/html.
configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.subtitle: Control plane and admin portal settings.
configuration.toast.restartSuccess: Frontend service restarted. Refresh the page if the UI does not reload.
configuration.toast.restartFailed: Could not restart the frontend service.
configuration.readOnlyHint: Only administrators can change portal access settings.
configuration.portalAccess.title: Admin portal access
configuration.portalAccess.description: Enter every URL you use to open this UI in the browser (scheme, host, and port). This updates API CORS and the Vite preview allowed-host list. The SPA calls the API on the same hostname as this page (port 3001 by default) unless you build with VITE_API_USE_FIXED — add each UI origin here for CORS.
configuration.portalAccess.label: Allowed origins (one per line)
configuration.portalAccess.help: Examples: http://192.168.1.10:8080, https://warden.example.com (include port if it is not 80/443).
configuration.systemUpdate.title: Update from GitHub
configuration.systemUpdate.description: Schedules a background job on the server: git fetch/pull (origin and your configured branch), then scripts/update.sh to install dependencies, migrate, rebuild, and restart services. The API returns immediately; follow progress in /var/log/nginx-warden-ui-update.log on the host. The API must run as root, same as a manual update.
configuration.systemUpdate.confirmDesc: This stops backend and frontend briefly, pulls from GitHub, rebuilds, and restarts. It can take several minutes. Ensure your branch can fast-forward (no local changes blocking git pull), or the pull step may fail.
configuration.systemUpdate.warningHint: Use only on the server that hosts Nginx Warden. Set NGINX_WARDEN_ROOT on the API if the install path is not detected automatically. Disable with ENABLE_WEB_SYSTEM_UPDATE=false if you do not want this action available in the UI.
configuration.systemUpdate.logTruncated: Showing the last portion of the log file.
configuration.systemUpdate.logUnavailabl: Could not load the log (the API may be restarting). Last successful content is kept below when available.
configuration.systemUpdate.logEmpty: No update log file on this host yet.
configuration.systemUpdate.logFileEmpty: Log file exists but is empty.
configuration.systemUpdate.toast.schedul: Update started in the background. Check the server log for progress.
configuration.portalAccess.restartHint: After saving, Apply Changes or restart the frontend service: sudo systemctl restart nginx-warden-frontend.

users.table.title: Users ({{count}})
users.roleSection.title: Role descriptions
users.role.adminDesc: Full system access: manage all domains, users, settings, and configurations. Can delete critical resources.
users.role.moderatorDesc: Can manage domains, SSL, ModSecurity rules, and view logs. Cannot manage users or delete critical resources.
users.role.viewerDesc: Read-only access: view all configurations, logs, and metrics. Cannot make any changes.

account.twoFactor.title: Two-factor authentication

account.twoFactor.enableHelp: Secure your account with time-based one-time passwords
account.twoFactor.scanAlert: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app to complete setup.
account.twoFactor.authDesc: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)
account.twoFactor.verifyDesc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to verify and enable 2FA.
account.twoFactor.backupDesc: Save these backup codes in a safe place. You can use them if you lose your authenticator device.
account.twoFactor.alertEnabled: Two-factor authentication is enabled. Toggle the switch above to disable it.
account.twoFactor.alertDisabled: Two-factor authentication is not enabled. Enable it for stronger security.
account.activity.title: Activity log
account.activity.desc: View your recent account activity and login history
account.sessions.title: Active sessions
account.sessions.desc: Devices and browsers signed in to your account. Revoking a session signs that device out.
force2fa.toast.notEnabledDesc: You skipped 2FA setup. You can enable it later in account settings.
force2fa.toast.enabledBody: Your account is now secured with two-factor authentication!
force2fa.toast.skipBody: Your account is not protected by two-factor authentication
force2fa.setupError: Failed to set up 2FA. Please contact an administrator.
force2fa.skipDialog.title: Security warning
force2fa.skipDialog.intro: You are about to skip two-factor authentication setup.
force2fa.skipDialog.risk1: Your account will be more vulnerable to unauthorized access
force2fa.skipDialog.risk2: A password alone is not enough for strong security
force2fa.skipDialog.risk3: The risk of account compromise increases significantly
force2fa.skipDialog.confirmQuestion: Are you sure you want to continue without 2FA?
force2fa.title: Enable two-factor authentication
force2fa.subtitle: Add an extra layer of protection to your account
force2fa.infoAlert: Two-factor authentication greatly improves security by requiring both your password and a verification code.
force2fa.step1.title: Step 1: Scan QR code
force2fa.step1.desc: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, Microsoft Authenticator, etc.)
force2fa.step2.title: Step 2: Save backup codes
force2fa.step2.desc: Store these backup codes somewhere safe. You can use them to sign in if you lose your device.
force2fa.step3.title: Step 3: Verify setup
force2fa.step3.desc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to verify and enable 2FA.
force2fa.footerNote: After verification, you can access the system with your password and 2FA enabled.

Nginx has been reloaded.
backup.toast.restoreFailed: Failed to restore configuration. Please check the file format.
backup.page.title: Backup & restore
backup.page.subtitle: Manage configuration backups and restore points
backup.export.title: Export configuration
backup.export.body: Export all domains, SSL certificates, ModSecurity rules, and system settings to a JSON file.
backup.import.title: Import configuration
backup.import.body: Import and restore configuration from a previously exported backup file.
backup.schedules.title: Scheduled backups
backup.schedules.dialogDesc: Configure automatic backups using cron syntax
backup.schedules.name: Backup name
backup.table.name: Name
backup.info.title: Backup information
backup.info.desc: Important notes about backup and restore
backup.info.whatBody: All domain configurations, SSL certificates, ModSecurity rules, ACL rules, alert settings, and system preferences.
backup.info.processBody: Importing a backup will merge configurations. Existing items with the same ID will be updated.
backup.info.bestBody: Keep multiple backup versions, test restore procedures regularly, and store backups in a secure location.
backup.delete.title: Delete backup schedule
backup.delete.desc: Are you sure you want to delete this backup schedule? This action cannot be undone.
backup.importDialog.title: Import configuration backup
backup.importDialog.criticalTitle: Critical warning — all data will be replaced
backup.importDialog.criticalBody: Importing a backup will completely replace all existing configurations on this server. This is irreversible without a prior backup.
backup.importDialog.before1: Export your current configuration as a safety backup
backup.importDialog.before2: Ensure the backup file is from a trusted source
backup.importDialog.before3: Verify the backup file is not corrupted
backup.importDialog.before4: Notify other administrators about the restore
backup.importDialog.dropHint: Click to browse or drag and drop
backup.confirm.title: Confirm configuration restore
backup.confirm.warningBody: Restoring this backup will replace all existing data on this server with data from the backup file.
backup.confirm.item1: Domains: all domain configurations, upstreams, load balancers
backup.confirm.item2: Nginx configs: virtual host files in sites-available
backup.confirm.item3: SSL certificates: certificate files in nginx ssl directories
backup.confirm.item4: ModSecurity rules: CRS and custom security rules
backup.confirm.item5: ACL rules: all access control configurations
backup.confirm.item6: Alert settings: notification channels and alert rules
backup.confirm.after4: Users can sign in with passwords from the backup
backup.confirm.recommend: Recommendation: export your current configuration before restoring.
backup.confirm.question: Do you want to proceed with the restore?

alerts.rules.title: Alert rules
alerts.rules.desc: Define conditions and thresholds for alerts
alerts.rules.hint.cpu: Alert when CPU usage exceeds the threshold
alerts.rules.hint.memory: Alert when memory usage exceeds the threshold
alerts.rules.hint.disk: Alert when disk usage exceeds the threshold
alerts.rules.hint.upstream: Alert when any upstream/backend is down
alerts.rules.hint.ssl: Alert when an SSL certificate expires soon
alerts.rules.thresholdHintPercent: Alert when usage exceeds this percentage
alerts.rules.thresholdHintDays: Alert when the certificate expires within this many days
alerts.rules.checkHintGeneral: How often to check (10–86400s). CPU/memory: 30s, disk: 300s
alerts.rules.deleteDesc: Delete “{{name}}”? You will stop receiving alerts for this condition.
acl.toast.importMockDesc: Select a JSON file to import rules
nlbForm.toast.validationDetail: Please fix {{count}} validation issue(s) before submitting.
nlbForm.toast.errNameExists: An NLB with this name already exists. Choose a different name.
nlbForm.toast.errInvalidHost: Invalid upstream host. Check the IP address or hostname format.
nlbForm.toast.saveFailedGeneric: Could not save the load balancer. Check your configuration and try again.
nlbForm.validation.nameMin: Name must be at least 3 characters
nlbForm.validation.nameChars: Name can only contain letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores
nlbForm.validation.nameEnds: Name cannot start or end with a dash or underscore
nlbForm.validation.hostInvalid: Invalid host. Enter a valid IP address (IPv4/IPv6) or hostname
nlbForm.validation.maxFailsRange: Max fails must be between 0 and 100
nlbForm.validation.failTimeoutRange: Fail timeout must be between 1 and 3600 seconds
nlbForm.validation.maxConnsRange: Max connections must be between 0 and 100000
nlbForm.validation.upstreamsRequired: At least one upstream is required
nlbForm.validation.upstreamsNoActive: At least one upstream must be active (not marked as down or backup only)
nlbForm.validation.proxyTimeoutRange: Proxy timeout must be between 1 and 3600 seconds
nlbForm.validation.proxyConnectTimeoutRa: Proxy connect timeout must be between 1 and 300 seconds
nlbForm.validation.proxyNextUpstreamTime: Next upstream timeout must be between 0 and 3600 seconds
nlbForm.validation.proxyNextUpstreamTrie: Next upstream tries must be between 0 and 100
nlbForm.validation.healthCheckIntervalRa: Health check interval must be between 5 and 3600 seconds
nlbForm.validation.healthCheckTimeoutRan: Health check timeout must be between 1 and 300 seconds
nlbForm.validation.healthCheckRisesRange: Health check rises must be between 1 and 10
nlbForm.validation.healthCheckFallsRange: Health check falls must be between 1 and 10
nlbForm.validation.healthTimeoutLtInterv: Health check timeout must be less than the interval
nlbForm.validation.proxyConnectLtProxy: Proxy connect timeout should be less than proxy timeout
nlbForm.fields.name: Name
nlbForm.rhf.upstreamsRequired: At least one upstream is required
nlbForm.hints.name: Use 3–50 characters: letters, numbers, dashes, underscores
nlbForm.hints.port: Port must be 10000–65535 to avoid conflicts
nlbForm.hints.host: Enter an IP address (IPv4/IPv6) or hostname
nlbForm.hints.maxFails: Failed attempts before marking the server down (0–100)
nlbForm.hints.failTimeout: Wait time before retrying a failed server (1–3600s)
nlbForm.hints.proxyTimeout: Max time to wait for an upstream response (1–3600s)
nlbForm.hints.proxyConnectTimeout: Max time to establish a connection (1–300s)
nlbForm.hints.healthCheckTimeout: Max time to wait for a health check response (1–300s)
nlbForm.examples.name: my-load-balancer
nlbForm.warnings.sameWeight: All upstreams use the same weight. Consider weight 1 for simplicity.
nlbForm.warnings.proxyTimeoutHigh: Proxy timeout is very high (>5 minutes). Clients may wait a long time.
nlbForm.warnings.healthDisabled: Health checks are disabled. Failed upstreams will not be detected automatically.
nlbForm.warnings.healthFrequent: Health check interval is very frequent (<10s). This may increase load.
nlbForm.warnings.backupOnly: Only backup servers are configured. They are used when all primary servers are down.
nlbForm.description: Configure a Layer 4 load balancer for TCP/UDP traffic.
nlbForm.label.name: Name
nlbForm.tooltip.backup: Used only when all primary servers are down
nlbForm.tooltip.markDown: Mark this server unavailable (maintenance or error)

accessLists.hint.username: Use only letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens (3–50 characters)
